AMZN.reasoning: 1. MACRO REGIME: Risk-on with near-term caveats. S&P 500 at fresh all-time highs above 7,200; April 2026 was the best month since 2020. Fed held at 3.5%-3.75% in an 8-4 vote (most dissents since Oct 1992), introducing policy uncertainty ahead of the Powell-to-Warsh transition on May 15. Iran peace talks have WTI -2%, removing the dominant left-tail energy-inflation risk. AI capex supercycle narrative is intact — Amazon beat on Q1 cloud revenue — but MSFT post-earnings selloff and Meta -9% capex shock injected near-term AI spend uncertainty. Net read: risk-on regime, macro tailwind for AMZN's AWS/AI story, but with elevated policy uncertainty. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: AMZN is decisively outperforming. ROC 21d at +27.27% vs broad market at fresh highs — this is sector leadership, not just market beta. EMA aligned uptrend confirmed on both daily and hourly timeframes. ADX 64.34 with +DI 45.78 vs -DI 11.51 confirms a very strong, uncontested trend. However, outperformance at this magnitude (+27% in 21 days) flags a late-cycle entry, not an early-cycle accumulation. 3. ASYMMETRIC R/R (EXPLICIT NUMBERS): Entry at $269. Target at hourly Fib ext 1.272 ($277.98) = +3.3% upside. Stop at volume-confirmed hourly pivot support ($260.34) = -3.2% downside. Ratio: 1.03:1 — this fails the 2:1 minimum bar. For a 2:1 ratio, upside would need to be $278.78+ or stop would need to tighten to ~$264, but $264 is already above EMA-aligned support levels and would be too tight given 2.68% daily ATR. Even using the daily Fib ext 1.272 ($294.21) as target = +9.4% upside vs -3.2% downside = 2.94:1, but that target requires holding 15-20 trading days through an earnings risk window, and the thesis does not support chasing at RSI 88+. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION FLAGS: RSI 14 = 82.21, RSI 21 = 88.83 — extremely overbought, identical regime to the NVDA trade (RSI 81-87) that triggered a stop loss per wiki lessons. Hourly OBV is diverging bearishly from price, and hourly RSI shows bearish divergence — early sector-rotation warning signals. Daily volume was 2x average on April 30, but the daily candle was a bearish reversal: opened $273.17, closed $265.06 (well off the $273.88 high). Z-score vs 50 SMA = 2.01 (historically stretched). 18.58% above 50 SMA is an extreme extension. WIKI LESSON ENFORCEMENT: The lessons_full Patterns table shows 0% win rate on EMA pullback Fib dip-buy in last 30 days, and the NVDA loss explicitly notes 'stretched macro (RSI 81-87) with no bullish confirmation candle' as the cause. AMZN is in the same RSI regime. This is a wait-for-pullback, not a chase.
